workspace.name: IDIOMEX OS V2
workspace.subtitle: Business Operating System · Executive + Operator Views
workspace.welcome: IDIOMEX OS V2 now separates strategic CEO decisions from execution-heavy COO operations.
workspace.status: Version 2 upgraded with executive cockpit, richer CRM, and automation queue

sync_status.note: This V2 dashboard is accessible anywhere once hosted, but edits still stay in this browser until a backend such as Supabase is added.

ceo.label: CEO view
ceo.summary: Use this view to decide where cash, attention, and positioning should go next. It compresses the business into the three decisions that matter now: what to sell first, what to ship next, and what can be delegated to automation.

cards.label: Capacity release
cards.delta: If outreach + reporting automations stay active

priorities: Turn the MTD opportunity into a simple, sellable one-page offer with clear setup + monthly support pricing.
Finish Cycle Matter onboarding so it becomes a reusable template for future business-agent installs.
Keep IDIOMEX OS focused on one-screen executive clarity instead of turning it into a bloated app too early.
decisions: Which single vertical gets the first outreach sprint: accountants/bookkeepers, trades, or local service firms?
Do you add Supabase next for true sync, or keep V2 static and use export/import until demand proves it?
What should become a productized service first: agent onboarding, outreach automation, or dashboard implementation?

coo.label: COO view
coo.summary: Use this view to run delivery. It focuses on the next operational bottlenecks: task flow, owners, blockers, and which automations are actively taking admin off your plate.

cards.label: Tasks in flight
cards.delta: Keep no more than three active at once

priorities: Complete Cycle Matter knowledge docs and SOUL.md before adding more client-agent complexity.
Move all high-priority tasks with due dates into a tighter operating cadence and keep the kanban accurate.
Define the data contract for a future sync backend so the static dashboard can upgrade cleanly.
decisions: Which tasks can be auto-created by agent outputs instead of being entered manually?
What minimum schema is needed for synced entities: leads, tasks, projects, activity, automation runs?
Which cron jobs deserve user-facing surfaces inside the dashboard versus staying invisible back-office flows?

kpis.label: Live automations
kpis.delta: Taking repeat admin off the board

summary.headline: Best current opportunity: MTD onboarding and quarterly-support automation.
summary.note: The strongest V2 CRM move is to track not just ideas, but offer, owner, close target, and blockers.

leads.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and quarterly-support automation
leads.buyer: Sole traders, landlords, bookkeepers, small accountancy firms
leads.offer: Setup fee + monthly quarterly filing support
leads.next_action: Draft one-page offer and shortlist 30 local accountants/bookkeepers
leads.blocker: Need pricing anchor and first outreach list
leads.notes: Compliance trigger is already live, which makes this easier to sell than broad AI consulting.

leads.name: Invoice chasing and e-invoicing automation for UK SMEs
leads.buyer: Micro businesses, agencies, consultancies, trades firms
leads.next_action: Build ROI framing around reduced debtor days and collected cash
leads.blocker: Need a sharper before/after value example
leads.notes: A very good cash-flow story if positioned around money recovered rather than tech.

leads.name: AI lead-response and admin automation for trades
leads.buyer: Local service businesses: plumbers, electricians, clinics, salons
leads.next_action: Pick one vertical and define a before/after ROI case study
leads.blocker: Too broad without one vertical first
leads.notes: Strong only when sold as recovered revenue and saved admin, not generic automation.

leads.name: Cycle Matter onboarding productization
leads.buyer: Local owner-managed businesses that need Telegram-first agents
leads.next_action: Turn current onboarding work into a repeatable checklist and demo narrative
leads.blocker: Needs one completed business case study
leads.notes: Promising because you are already doing the work once for real.

leads.name: Tender monitoring and bid-drafting assistant
leads.next_action: Choose one supplier vertical and test interest before building
leads.blocker: Longer sales cycle than compliance-led offers
leads.notes: Interesting niche, but not the fastest route to cash right now.

leads.name: Cyber Essentials readiness workflow automation
leads.buyer: Small professional firms, agencies, clinics, charities
leads.next_action: Package as a fixed-scope ops checklist rather than bespoke security consulting
leads.blocker: Positioning risk if it sounds too security-heavy
leads.notes: Viable, but weaker than the top two opportunities on urgency and clarity.

projects.name: Cycle Matter agent
projects.focus: Onboarding, business context, and first live business-grade behavior
projects.next_milestone: Finalize SOUL.md and complete business knowledge base

projects.name: IDIOMEX OS V2
projects.focus: Unified dashboard with executive cockpit and operations view
projects.next_milestone: Add backend-ready schema for synced leads/tasks/projects

projects.name: Hermes agent setup system
projects.focus: Reusable setup guide and workflow for future business agents
projects.next_milestone: Turn current install path into a repeatable onboarding playbook
projects.blocker: Needs a fully documented end-to-end client example

tasks.title: Cycle Matter agent onboarding
tasks.notes: Main active task. Finish the onboarding process and structure the business context.

tasks.title: Write Cycle Matter business knowledge docs
tasks.notes: Document services, FAQs, policies, pricing ranges, and common customer situations.

tasks.title: Update Cycle Matter SOUL.md
tasks.notes: Define role, tone, guardrails, escalation rules, and business priorities.

tasks.title: Test Cycle Matter onboarding scenarios
tasks.automation: Replay prompts once knowledge docs land
tasks.notes: Run realistic prompts for enquiries, pricing questions, complaints, and escalations.

tasks.title: Define synced entity schema for IDIOMEX OS
tasks.notes: Design the minimum schema for leads, tasks, projects, activity, and automation events.

tasks.title: Map Supabase upgrade path
tasks.notes: Prepare the next step from static dashboard to synced backend without overbuilding.

tasks.title: Finalize reusable onboarding workflow
tasks.notes: Turn setup + onboarding into a cleaner productized system for future client agents.

tasks.title: Create agent setup examples pack
tasks.automation: Can be generated from first 2 client installs
tasks.notes: Collect examples for service, trades, and internal business assistants.

tasks.title: Publish IDIOMEX OS publicly
tasks.notes: Current public dashboard is already accessible online.

automation.summary: The goal for V2 is not to automate everything; it is to make repeat work visible, dependable, and easy to improve.

items.action: Send practical Telegram briefing with tool/tutorial-of-the-day
items.outcome: Keeps AI trends, coding tools, and agent workflows in one reliable morning surface.

items.action: Push Hermes data backup to GitHub
items.outcome: Reduces risk of losing configuration, prompts, and dashboard artifacts.

items.action: Collect and summarize live topic research for business/operator decisions
items.outcome: Cuts time to insight when exploring offers, markets, and AI tooling.

items.action: Prepare next-action prompts and outreach sequencing
items.outcome: Should reduce the jump from research to actual outreach execution.

activity.title: IDIOMEX OS V2 upgraded
activity.detail: Executive cockpit, automation queue, and richer CRM metadata added to the dashboard.

activity.title: Cycle Matter moved deeper into onboarding
activity.detail: Focus narrowed to SOUL.md, knowledge docs, and realistic scenario testing.

activity.title: Public dashboard deployment verified
activity.detail: GitHub Pages version confirmed reachable from anywhere.

activity.title: Hermes setup guide refined
activity.detail: Reusable setup path becoming a future productized service asset.

today: Finish the Cycle Matter onboarding structure and core docs
Lock the first sellable MTD offer framing
Define the synced data schema for a future backend
